sections.name: Executive Summary
sections.content:
## Executive Summary

### Business Problem
RetailCorp India, a mid-size retail chain operating approximately 200 physical stores across India, currently has no online sales channel. With high-ticket product categories including electronics, home appliances, and furniture averaging INR 15,000 per order, the absence of an e-commerce presence represents a significant missed revenue opportunity in a competitive retail market.

### Proposed Solution
ShopEase is a greenfield e-commerce platform purpose-built for RetailCorp India, delivering a full-featured online storefront with integrated payments, logistics, and vendor management. The platform will connect end customers, store managers, vendors, and central administrators through role-specific portals, with deep integration into RetailCorp's existing SAP ERP system.

### Key Objectives
- Launch a fully operational e-commerce platform within 6 months of project kickoff
- Enable multi-method payments including UPI, cards, net banking, and EMI via Bajaj Finance
- Integrate real-time inventory sync with SAP ERP to ensure accurate product availability
- Onboard vendor partners with a self-service portal, scaling the catalog from 8,000 to 25,000 SKUs
- Deliver sales analytics and cart abandonment reporting to support data-driven commercial decisions

### Scope Overview
- **Customer Experience:** Product search, filtering, wishlist, cart, checkout, order tracking, returns, and reviews via web and native mobile apps (Android and iOS)
- **Integrations:** SAP ERP (inventory and product data), Delhivery and BlueDart (logistics and tracking), Bajaj Finance (EMI); ZestMoney integration subject to commercial agreement
- **Administration and Vendor Management:** Role-based portals for central admins, store managers, and vendor partners; centralized pricing control retained by RetailCorp
- **Compliance and Infrastructure:** GDPR and Indian IT Act compliance, full data residency within India, PCI-DSS payment processing, and elastic cloud infrastructure supporting 50,000 concurrent users

### Expected Outcomes
ShopEase will establish RetailCorp India's first direct-to-consumer online channel, enabling nationwide sales beyond the physical store footprint. The platform is designed to handle peak traffic events such as Diwali sales while maintaining sub-3-second page load performance. Vendor onboarding capability positions RetailCorp to scale its catalog and revenue streams beyond its own inventory.

sections.name: Business Context and Background
sections.content:
## Business Context and Background

### Current State

RetailCorp India Pvt. is a mid-size retail chain operating approximately 200 physical stores across India, currently managing around 8,000 SKUs through its SAP ERP system. The organization has no existing e-commerce presence; all sales and customer engagement occur exclusively through brick-and-mortar channels. Product catalog management, inventory, and financial operations are handled entirely within the SAP ERP environment.

### Business Problem

RetailCorp India requires a greenfield e-commerce platform — ShopEase — to establish a direct online sales channel, expand market reach beyond physical store footprints, and support vendor onboarding that will scale the catalog from 8,000 to approximately 25,000 SKUs. The platform must integrate with SAP ERP, third-party logistics providers (Delhivery and BlueDart), and financing partners (Bajaj Finance, with ZestMoney pending commercial agreement) to deliver a complete transactional experience including No-Cost EMI payment options. Additionally, all customer data must be stored exclusively on India-based servers to satisfy regulatory data residency requirements, constraining infrastructure decisions to cloud providers with verified Indian data center presence.

### Cost of Inaction

Failure to deliver ShopEase within the CEO-mandated six-month deadline will leave RetailCorp India without a competitive online channel, resulting in continued revenue loss to digitally-native competitors and missed vendor partnership opportunities tied to the platform launch. Delayed delivery also risks the lapse of the confirmed Bajaj Finance commercial agreement and jeopardizes RetailCorp's ability to offer No-Cost EMI at launch — a key customer acquisition lever in the Indian e-commerce market.

sections.name: Project Objectives and Success Criteria
sections.content:
## Project Objectives and Success Criteria

### Business Objectives

1. Launch ShopEase as a fully operational greenfield e-commerce channel for RetailCorp India's 200-store retail network, achieving platform go-live within the agreed project timeline.
2. Migrate and publish the full 8,000-SKU SAP ERP catalog at launch, scaling to 25,000 SKUs within 6 months of vendor onboarding completion.
3. Deliver a platform capable of supporting 50,000 concurrent users during peak sale events (e.g., Diwali) with zero critical downtime incidents.
4. Achieve P95 page load times under 3 seconds across all key pages (home, PDP, cart, checkout) under both normal and peak traffic conditions.
5. Ensure full regulatory compliance across GDPR, Indian IT Act, and PCI-DSS standards, with 100% of required compliance acceptance criteria met at launch.
6. Integrate confirmed payment financing partner (Bajaj Finance) at launch, with ZestMoney integration delivered within 90 days of commercial agreement execution.
7. Maintain SAP ERP inventory sync latency under 5 minutes with a minimum integration uptime of 99.5%, eliminating stock discrepancy risks across all channels.

### Success Criteria

| Objective | Metric | Target |
|---|---|---|
| Platform go-live | Launch milestone achieved | On-time delivery |
| Catalog scalability | Active SKUs supported | 25,000 SKUs within 6 months |
| Peak traffic capacity | Concurrent users under load test | 50,000 with zero critical errors |
| Page performance | P95 page load time | < 3 seconds at peak load |
| Regulatory compliance | Compliance ACs passed | 100% at launch |
| Payment integration | Bajaj Finance live at launch; ZestMoney post-launch | Within 90 days of agreement |
| SAP sync reliability | Sync latency + uptime | ≤ 5 min latency; ≥ 99.5% uptime |

sections.name: Scope
sections.content:
## Scope

### In Scope

- Greenfield e-commerce platform (ShopEase) built for RetailCorp India's online retail channel
- Product catalog management supporting ~8,000 SKUs at launch, scalable to 25,000 SKUs post vendor onboarding
- SAP ERP integration for catalog, inventory, and order data synchronization
- Multi-role user management: Central Admin Team, Store Manager, and Vendor Partner access tiers
- Third-party logistics API integration with Delhivery and BlueDart for order fulfillment and tracking
- No-Cost EMI payment option via confirmed Bajaj Finance integration
- Cart Abandonment Analytics reporting for customer behavior insights
- Customer product review submission and Review Moderation Workflow
- Cloud infrastructure deployment exclusively within an Indian data center region (data residency compliance per TR-025, TR-040)
- Customer PII storage and processing restricted to India-based servers
- Platform go-live within 6 months of project kickoff (hard deadline per TR-001)

### Out of Scope

- ZestMoney No-Cost EMI integration — excluded from Phase 1 launch; contingent on pending commercial agreement (TR-033)
- Integration with any logistics provider beyond Delhivery and BlueDart
- Migration of existing offline/in-store transaction history or loyalty data
- Physical store POS system integration or in-store digital touchpoints
- Modifications to the existing SAP ERP system configuration
- International shipping, multi-currency support, or cross-border commerce capabilities
- Mobile native application (iOS/Android) — web platform only in Phase 1

### Future Scope / Phase 2

- ZestMoney No-Cost EMI integration, subject to commercial agreement finalization
- Native mobile application for iOS and Android
- Vendor self-onboarding portal with automated SKU bulk upload capabilities
- Advanced personalization and AI-driven product recommendation engine
- Expansion of logistics provider network beyond current confirmed partners

sections.name: Stakeholder Register
sections.content:
## Stakeholder Register

| Name | Role | Organisation | Responsibilities | Involvement |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Sunita Agarwal | Client CEO / Executive Sponsor | RetailCorp India Pvt | Defines business vision, priorities, and platform scope; final decision-making authority | High — key decision maker; source of business requirements |
| Rajesh Mehta | Client Project Manager | RetailCorp India Pvt | Defines technical and operational requirements; acts as primary client contact | High — day-to-day client liaison; requirements owner |
| Arjun Shah | Business Analyst | Delivery Team | Elicits, documents, and manages project requirements | High — core delivery team member; requirements documentation |
| Priya Nair | Project Manager | Delivery Team | Project planning, delivery coordination, and integration scoping | High — core delivery team member; delivery oversight |
| Central Admin Team | Platform Administrators | RetailCorp India Pvt | Manages all products, pricing, and promotions across the ShopEase platform; holds highest level of administrative access | Medium — internal operational users; platform configuration |
| Store Manager | Regional User | RetailCorp India Pvt | Views regional orders and manages returns for their assigned physical store location | Medium — regional operational users; order and returns management |
| Vendor Partner | Third-Party Seller | External / Third Party | Lists products on the ShopEase platform; manages own inventory and product information via dedicated login | Medium — external platform users; product and inventory management |
| End Customer | Primary Platform User | External / Public | Browses the product catalog and places orders on the ShopEase platform | High — primary end users; core audience for platform delivery |

---

The communication plan for ShopEase-2 will follow a structured cadence: weekly status updates shared with Rajesh Mehta as primary client contact, with escalations and scope decisions directed to Sunita Agarwal as executive sponsor. Delivery team members Arjun Shah and Priya Nair will maintain internal daily stand-ups and document all requirement changes through a formal change log. Vendor Partners, Store Managers, and the Central Admin Team will be engaged during UAT and training phases via targeted communications coordinated through Rajesh Mehta.

sections.name: Functional Requirements
sections.content:
# Functional Requirements ## Project: ShopEase-2 | Client: RetailCorp | Document Section: 4.0 --- ### Customer Registration & Authentication | FR-ID | Requirement (The system SHALL...) | Priority | Acceptance Criteria | |-------|-----------------------------------|----------|---------------------| | FR-001 | allow new customers to register using a valid email address, creating a unique account upon successful verification. | MUST HAVE | • Customer can register using a valid email address
• Duplicate email registration is rejected with an appropriate error message
• Unique account is created upon successful verification | | FR-002 | allow new customers to register using a valid mobile number, creating a unique account upon successful verification. | MUST HAVE | • Customer can register using a valid mobile number
• Duplicate mobile number registration is rejected with an appropriate error message
• Unique account is created upon successful verification | | FR-003 | allow registered customers to log in using their registered email address and password. | MUST HAVE | • Customer can log in with a registered email and correct password
• Invalid credentials display a clear error message without revealing which field is incorrect | | FR-004 | allow registered customers to log in using a One-Time Password (OTP) sent to their registered mobile number or email address. | MUST HAVE | • Customer can log in using an OTP sent to their registered mobile number or email
• Both authentication methods are available at all times
• Invalid OTP displays a clear error message | --- ### User Roles & Access Control | FR-ID | Requirement (The system SHALL...) | Priority | Acceptance Criteria | |-------|-----------------------------------|----------|---------------------| | FR-005 | support four distinct user roles — end customer, store manager, central admin, and vendor partner — each with a separate login portal. | MUST HAVE | • Each role can log in with role-specific credentials
• Each role sees only the features and data relevant to their role
• Unauthorized access to another role's portal is blocked | | FR-006 | restrict vendor portal access so that vendors cannot view, set, or modify any product pricing fields. | MUST HAVE | • Vendor portal does not expose any pricing fields
• Vendor cannot set or modify product prices through any portal function | --- ### Product Catalog Management | FR-ID | Requirement (The system SHALL...) | Priority | Acceptance Criteria | |-------|-----------------------------------|----------|---------------------| | FR-007 | allow central admins to add a new product to the catalog with all required fields including name, brand, category, description, images, price, and SKU. | MUST HAVE | • Admin can add a new product with all required fields (name, brand, category, description, images, price, SKU)
• New product is visible to customers upon activation | | FR-008 | allow central admins to edit any field of an existing product in the catalog. | MUST HAVE | • Admin can edit any field of an existing product
• Changes are reflected on the product page immediately upon save | | FR-009 | allow central admins to deactivate a product, making it invisible to customers without permanently deleting the product record. | MUST HAVE | • Admin can deactivate a product
• Deactivated product is not visible to customers
• Product record is retained and can be reactivated | | FR-010 | allow central admins to set a base price and configure discounts (percentage or fixed amount) for each product, including start and end dates for time-limited discounts. | MUST HAVE | • Admin can set a base price for each product
• Admin can create a discount as a percentage or fixed amount
• Admin can set a start and end date for time-limited discounts
• Customers always see the current correct pricing | | FR-011 | display a detailed product page to customers including name, images with zoom capability, description, specifications, price, availability, and customer reviews. | MUST HAVE | • Product page displays product name, brand, and category
• At least one product image is shown with zoom capability
• Product description, key specifications, price, and availability are displayed
• Approved customer reviews are visible on the product page | --- ### Product Search & Discovery | FR-ID | Requirement (The system SHALL...) | Priority | Acceptance Criteria | |-------|-----------------------------------|----------|---------------------| | FR-012 | provide a product search capability accessible from all pages, allowing customers to search by product name, brand, and category, returning results in a ranked and relevant order. | MUST HAVE | • Search bar is accessible from all pages of the platform
• Search returns relevant results when queried by product name
• Search returns relevant results when queried by category
• Search returns relevant results when queried by brand | | FR-013 | allow customers to filter search results by price range, brand, customer rating, and estimated delivery time, with multiple filters applicable simultaneously. | MUST HAVE | • Customer can filter results by a minimum and maximum price range
• Customer can filter results by one or more brands
• Customer can filter results by minimum star rating
• Customer can filter results by estimated delivery time
• Multiple filters can be applied simultaneously | --- ### Shopping Cart | FR-ID | Requirement (The system SHALL...) | Priority | Acceptance Criteria | |-------|-----------------------------------|----------|---------------------| | FR-014 | allow customers to add one or more products to a shopping cart from the product detail page or search results for review and purchase in a single transaction. | MUST HAVE | • Customer can add a product to the cart from the product detail page or search results
• Cart displays all added products with name, quantity, unit price, and subtotal | | FR-015 | allow customers to update the quantity of items in their shopping cart. | MUST HAVE | • Customer can increase or decrease the quantity of any item in the cart
• Cart totals update automatically upon quantity change | --- ### Wishlist | FR-ID | Requirement (The system SHALL...) | Priority | Acceptance Criteria | |-------|-----------------------------------|----------|---------------------| | FR-016 | allow registered customers to add products to a wishlist from the product page or search results for future reference, accessible from their account dashboard. | SHOULD HAVE | • Logged-in customer can add a product to their wishlist from the product page or search results
• Wishlist is accessible from the customer's account dashboard
• Customer can view all wishlisted products | | FR-017 | allow customers to remove products from their wishlist. | MUST HAVE | • Customer can remove any product from their wishlist
• Removed product no longer appears in the wishlist | --- ### Checkout & Delivery Address | FR-ID | Requirement (The system SHALL...) | Priority | Acceptance Criteria | |-------|-----------------------------------|----------|---------------------| | FR-018 | allow customers to add multiple delivery addresses to their account and select one saved address during checkout. | MUST HAVE | • Customer can add a new delivery address with full address fields (name, street, city, state, PIN code, phone)
• Customer can save multiple delivery addresses to their account
• Customer can select a saved address during checkout | --- ### Payment Processing | FR-ID | Requirement (The system SHALL...) | Priority | Acceptance Criteria | |-------|-----------------------------------|----------|---------------------| | FR-019 | support credit card, debit card, UPI, and net banking as payment methods, all available at checkout at all times. | MUST HAVE | • Customer can complete payment using credit card
• Customer can complete payment using debit card
• Customer can complete payment using UPI
• Customer can complete payment using net banking | | FR-020 | support credit card EMI and no-cost EMI through Bajaj Finance and ZestMoney financing partners via API integration, displaying tenure and interest details at checkout. | MUST HAVE | • Customer can select credit card EMI at checkout
• Customer can select no-cost EMI via Bajaj Finance at checkout
• EMI options are displayed with tenure and interest details
• API integration with Bajaj Finance and ZestMoney is functional | | FR-021 | process payments securely, display a confirmation upon success, and display a clear error message on failure allowing retry without loss of cart contents. | MUST HAVE | • Payment is processed securely and a confirmation is shown upon success
• Failed payments display a clear error message
• Cart contents are retained after a failed payment attempt | --- ### Order Management & Tracking | FR-ID | Requirement (The system SHALL...) | Priority | Acceptance Criteria | |-------|-----------------------------------|----------|---------------------| | FR-022 | provide customers with real-time order tracking displaying current status, last known shipment checkpoint, and estimated delivery date and time, without requiring navigation to a third-party logistics site. | MUST HAVE | • Order detail page displays live tracking status fetched from logistics partner API
• Customer can view current order status (confirmed, packed, shipped, out for delivery, delivered)
• Tracking page displays the shipment's current location or last known checkpoint
• Estimated delivery date and time are shown and updated in real time
• Tracking status updates without requiring a page refresh or within a defined polling interval | | FR-023 | allow customers to initiate a return request for a delivered order within the return eligibility window by selecting a reason, and provide a confirmation with a reference number. | MUST HAVE | • Customer can initiate a return request from their order history for any delivered order within the return eligibility window
• Return request form requires the customer to select a reason (e.g., defective, not as described, wrong item)
• Customer receives a confirmation of the return request with a reference number | --- ### Product Reviews & Moderation | FR-ID | Requirement (The system SHALL...) | Priority | Acceptance Criteria | |-------|-----------------------------------|----------|---------------------| | FR-024 | allow customers with a confirmed delivered order to submit one written review and star rating (1–5) per purchased product, held in a pending state until approved. | SHOULD HAVE | • Only customers with a confirmed delivered order for the product can submit a review
• Review form includes a star rating (1–5) and a text field for written feedback
• Customer can submit only one review per purchased product
• Submitted review is held in pending state until approved by admin | | FR-025 | provide central admins with a review moderation interface to approve or reject pending customer-submitted reviews, with approved reviews published publicly on the product page. | SHOULD HAVE | • Admin can view a list of all pending reviews with reviewer name, product, rating, and review text
• Admin can approve a review, making it publicly visible on the product page
• Admin can reject a review with a reason, preventing it from being published
• Review is visible to other customers only after admin approval | --- ### Vendor Portal | FR-ID | Requirement (The system SHALL...) | Priority | Acceptance Criteria | |-------|-----------------------------------|----------|---------------------| | FR-026 | provide vendors with a self-service portal to add new product listings with required details including name, description, images, and stock quantity. | MUST HAVE | • Vendor can log in to a dedicated vendor portal
• Vendor can add new product listings with required details (name, description, images, stock quantity)
• Vendor portal does not expose any pricing fields | | FR-027 | allow vendors to update product descriptions, images, and inventory quantities for their existing listings through the vendor portal. | MUST HAVE | • Vendor can update product descriptions and images
• Vendor can update inventory quantities
• Changes are reflected on the platform upon save | --- ### Store Manager Portal | FR-ID | Requirement (The system SHALL...) | Priority | Acceptance Criteria | |-------|-----------------------------------|----------|---------------------| | FR-028 | provide store managers with a dashboard scoped to their assigned region, displaying orders with order ID, customer name, items, order status, and delivery address. | SHOULD HAVE | • Store manager can log in and view a dashboard scoped to their assigned region
• Order list displays order ID, customer name, items, order status, and delivery address
• Store manager dashboard displays only orders from their assigned region | | FR-029 | allow store managers to filter regional orders by status and to initiate or approve return requests for orders within their region. | MUST HAVE | • Store manager can filter orders by status (e.g., pending, shipped, delivered, return requested)
• Store manager can initiate or approve return requests for orders in their region | --- ### SAP ERP Integration | FR-ID | Requirement (The system SHALL...) | Priority | Acceptance Criteria | |-------|-----------------------------------|----------|---------------------| | FR-030 | integrate with the client's SAP ERP system to sync inventory levels in real time, reflecting updates on the platform within a configurable sync interval not exceeding 5 minutes. | MUST HAVE | • Inventory levels are pulled from SAP and reflected on the platform within a configurable sync interval (default: real time or ≤ 5 minutes)
• Products with zero inventory are automatically marked as out of stock and cannot be added to cart
• Products returning to stock are automatically re-listed as available | | FR-031 | integrate with the client's SAP ERP system to sync product information via a daily batch process, logging and alerting on any sync failures. | MUST HAVE | • Product information is synced from SAP via daily batch process
• Sync failures are logged and alerted to the appropriate team | | FR-032 | support migration of all existing product data (approximately 8,000 SKUs) from the client's SAP ERP system prior to go-live, with validation for completeness and accuracy. | MUST HAVE | • All existing SAP product records are successfully imported into the platform before go-live
• Migrated product data is validated for completeness and accuracy
• Migration results report is produced confirming record counts and any exceptions | --- ### Logistics Integration | FR-ID | Requirement (The system SHALL...) | Priority | Acceptance Criteria | |-------|-----------------------------------|----------|---------------------| | FR-033 | integrate with Delhivery and BlueDart logistics APIs to fetch and display real-time shipping rates at checkout based on delivery address and order weight/dimensions. | MUST HAVE | • Shipping rates from Delhivery and BlueDart are fetched and displayed to the customer during checkout
• Rates are calculated based on delivery address and order weight/dimensions | | FR-034 | allow customers to select their preferred shipping carrier and service level at checkout, and automatically create a shipment with the selected carrier via API upon order confirmation. | MUST HAVE | • Customer can select their preferred shipping carrier and service level at checkout
• Upon order confirmation, a shipment is automatically created with the selected carrier via API | --- ### Reporting & Analytics | FR-ID | Requirement (The system SHALL...) | Priority | Acceptance Criteria | |-------|-----------------------------------|----------|---------------------| | FR-035 | provide central admins with sales reports filterable by daily, weekly, and monthly time periods, segmented by region, product category, and vendor, with export capability. | MUST HAVE | • Admin can generate a sales report filtered by date range (daily/weekly/monthly)
• Report can be segmented by region, category, and vendor
• Report data is exportable | | FR-036 | provide central admins with a cart abandonment analytics report showing products added to cart but not purchased, including add-to-cart count, purchase count, and abandonment rate, filterable by date range, category, and region. | SHOULD HAVE | • Admin can view a cart abandonment report listing products added to cart but not purchased
• Report shows product name, number of times added to cart, number of times purchased, and abandonment rate
• Report can be filtered by date range, category, and region
• Analytics dashboard displays products with high cart abandonment rates | | FR-037 | provide an executive mobile dashboard displaying key business metrics including total revenue, number of orders, and active users for configurable time periods, accessible via role-based access control. | SHOULD HAVE | • Executive dashboard is accessible via the mobile app with role-based access control
• Dashboard displays total revenue, number of orders, and active users for configurable time periods
• Metrics are presented in charts and KPI cards optimized for mobile screens | --- ### Marketing & Promotions | FR-ID | Requirement (The system SHALL...) | Priority | Acceptance Criteria | |-------|-----------------------------------|----------|---------------------| | FR-038 | allow central admins to create and send promotional email and SMS campaigns to defined customer segments, with scheduling for immediate or future delivery, via a built-in tool or third-party marketing platform integration. | SHOULD HAVE | • Admin can create a campaign with a subject, message body, and target audience segment

sections.name: Non-Functional Requirements
sections.content:
# Functional Requirements ## Project: ShopEase-2 | Client: RetailCorp | Document Section: 4.0 --- ### Customer Registration & Authentication | FR-ID | Requirement (The system SHALL...) | Priority | Acceptance Criteria | |-------|-----------------------------------|----------|---------------------| | FR-001 | allow new customers to register using a valid email address, creating a unique account upon successful verification. | MUST HAVE | • Customer can register using a valid email address
• Duplicate email registration is rejected with an appropriate error message
• Unique customer account is created upon successful verification | | FR-002 | allow new customers to register using a valid mobile number, creating a unique account upon successful verification. | MUST HAVE | • Customer can register using a valid mobile number
• Duplicate mobile number registration is rejected with an appropriate error message
• Unique customer account is created upon successful verification | | FR-003 | allow registered customers to log in using their registered email address and password. | MUST HAVE | • Customer can log in with a registered email and correct password
• Invalid credentials display a clear error message without revealing which field is incorrect | | FR-004 | allow registered customers to log in using a One-Time Password (OTP) sent to their registered mobile number or email address. | MUST HAVE | • Customer can log in using an OTP sent to their registered mobile number or email
• Invalid or expired OTP displays a clear error message | | FR-005 | support four distinct user roles — end customer, store manager, central admin, and vendor partner — each with a separate login portal. | MUST HAVE | • Each role can log in with role-specific credentials via a dedicated portal
• Each role sees only the features and data relevant to their role | --- ### Product Catalog & Search | FR-ID | Requirement (The system SHALL...) | Priority | Acceptance Criteria | |-------|-----------------------------------|----------|---------------------| | FR-006 | provide a product search capability, accessible from all pages, allowing customers to search by product name, category, and brand, returning results in a ranked and relevant order. | MUST HAVE | • Search bar is accessible from all pages of the platform
• Search returns relevant results when queried by product name
• Search returns relevant results when queried by category
• Search returns relevant results when queried by brand | | FR-007 | allow customers to filter search results by price range, brand, customer rating, and estimated delivery time, with multiple filters applicable simultaneously. | MUST HAVE | • Customer can filter results by minimum and maximum price range
• Customer can filter results by one or more brands
• Customer can filter results by minimum star rating
• Customer can filter results by estimated delivery time
• Multiple filters can be applied simultaneously | | FR-008 | display a detailed product page including product name, brand, category, images with zoom capability, description, specifications, price, availability, and customer reviews. | MUST HAVE | • Product page displays product name, brand, and category
• At least one product image is shown with zoom capability
• Product description, key specifications, price, and availability are displayed
• Approved customer reviews are visible on the product page | | FR-009 | allow central admins to add new products to the catalog with all required fields including name, brand, category, description, images, price, and SKU. | MUST HAVE | • Admin can add a new product with all required fields (name, brand, category, description, images, price, SKU)
• Newly added product is visible to customers upon activation | | FR-010 | allow central admins to edit any field of an existing product in the catalog. | MUST HAVE | • Admin can edit any field of an existing product
• Updated product information is reflected on the platform immediately upon save | | FR-011 | allow central admins to deactivate a product, making it invisible to customers without permanently deleting the product record. | MUST HAVE | • Admin can deactivate a product from the catalog
• Deactivated product is not visible to customers
• Deactivated product record is retained and can be reactivated | | FR-012 | allow central admins to set a base price and configure discounts (percentage or fixed amount) for each product, including start and end dates for time-limited discounts. | MUST HAVE | • Admin can set a base price for each product
• Admin can create a discount as a percentage or fixed amount
• Admin can set a start and end date for time-limited discounts
• Customers always see the current correct pricing | --- ### Shopping Cart & Wishlist | FR-ID | Requirement (The system SHALL...) | Priority | Acceptance Criteria | |-------|-----------------------------------|----------|---------------------| | FR-013 | allow customers to add one or more products to a shopping cart from the product detail page or search results, and update item quantities within the cart. | MUST HAVE | • Customer can add a product to the cart from the product detail page or search results
• Cart displays all added products with name, quantity, unit price, and subtotal
• Customer can update the quantity of items in the cart | | FR-014 | allow registered customers to save products to a wishlist accessible from their account dashboard, and remove products from the wishlist. | SHOULD HAVE | • Logged-in customer can add a product to their wishlist from the product page or search results
• Wishlist is accessible from the customer's account dashboard
• Customer can view all saved products in the wishlist
• Customer can remove a product from their wishlist | --- ### Checkout & Payment | FR-ID | Requirement (The system SHALL...) | Priority | Acceptance Criteria | |-------|-----------------------------------|----------|---------------------| | FR-015 | allow customers to add multiple delivery addresses to their account and select a saved address during checkout. | MUST HAVE | • Customer can add a new delivery address with full fields (name, street, city, state, PIN code, phone)
• Customer can save multiple delivery addresses to their account
• Customer can select a saved address during checkout | | FR-016 | support credit card, debit card, UPI, and net banking as payment methods, all available at checkout. | MUST HAVE | • Customer can complete payment using credit card
• Customer can complete payment using debit card
• Customer can complete payment using UPI
• Customer can complete payment using net banking
• All four methods are available at every checkout | | FR-017 | support credit card EMI and no-cost EMI through Bajaj Finance and ZestMoney financing partners via API integration, displaying tenure and interest details at checkout. | MUST HAVE | • Customer can select credit card EMI at checkout
• Customer can select no-cost EMI via Bajaj Finance at checkout
• EMI options are displayed with tenure and interest details
• System integrates with Bajaj Finance and ZestMoney APIs | | FR-018 | display shipping rates from Delhivery and BlueDart at checkout based on delivery address and order weight/dimensions, and allow the customer to select their preferred carrier and service level. | MUST HAVE | • Shipping rates from Delhivery and BlueDart are fetched and displayed during checkout
• Customer can select their preferred shipping carrier and service level
• Upon order confirmation, a shipment is automatically created with the selected carrier via API | | FR-019 | process payments securely, display a confirmation upon success, and display a clear error message on failure allowing retry without loss of cart contents. | MUST HAVE | • Payment is processed securely and a confirmation is shown upon success
• Failed payments display a clear error message
• Cart contents are retained after a failed payment attempt | --- ### Order Management & Tracking | FR-ID | Requirement (The system SHALL...) | Priority | Acceptance Criteria | |-------|-----------------------------------|----------|---------------------| | FR-020 | provide customers with real-time order tracking displaying current status, last known shipment checkpoint, and estimated delivery date and time, without requiring navigation to a third-party logistics site. | MUST HAVE | • Order detail page displays live tracking status fetched from logistics partner API
• Customer can view current order status (confirmed, packed, shipped, out for delivery, delivered)
• Tracking page displays the shipment's current location or last known checkpoint
• Estimated delivery date and time are shown and updated in real time
• Tracking status updates without requiring a page refresh or within a defined polling interval | | FR-021 | allow customers to initiate a return request for a delivered order within the return eligibility window by selecting a reason, and provide a confirmation with a reference number. | MUST HAVE | • Customer can initiate a return request from order history for any delivered order within the eligibility window
• Return request form requires selection of a reason (e.g., defective, not as described, wrong item)
• Customer receives a confirmation of the return request with a reference number | | FR-022 | allow store managers to view all orders within their assigned region, filtered by order status, and initiate or approve return requests for those orders. | MUST HAVE | • Store manager dashboard displays only orders from their assigned region
• Order list displays order ID, customer name, items, order status, and delivery address
• Store manager can filter orders by status (pending, shipped, delivered, return requested)
• Store manager can initiate or approve return requests | --- ### Reviews & Moderation | FR-ID | Requirement (The system SHALL...) | Priority | Acceptance Criteria | |-------|-----------------------------------|----------|---------------------| | FR-023 | allow customers with a confirmed delivered order to submit one written review and star rating (1–5) per purchased product, held in a pending state until moderated. | SHOULD HAVE | • Only customers with a confirmed delivered order for the product can submit a review
• Review form includes a star rating (1–5) and a text field for written feedback
• Customer can submit only one review per purchased product
• Submitted review is held in pending state until approved by admin | | FR-024 | provide central admins with a review moderation interface to approve or reject pending customer-submitted reviews, with approved reviews published on the product page. | SHOULD HAVE | • Admin can view a list of all pending reviews with reviewer name, product, rating, and review text
• Admin can approve a review, making it publicly visible on the product page
• Admin can reject a review with a reason, preventing it from being published | --- ### Vendor Management | FR-ID | Requirement (The system SHALL...) | Priority | Acceptance Criteria | |-------|-----------------------------------|----------|---------------------| | FR-025 | provide vendors with a self-service portal to add new product listings with required details including name, description, images, and stock quantity, without exposing any pricing fields. | MUST HAVE | • Vendor can log in to a dedicated vendor portal
• Vendor can add new product listings with required details (name, description, images, stock quantity)
• Vendor portal does not expose any pricing fields | | FR-026 | allow vendors to edit existing product listing details including descriptions, images, and inventory quantities through their portal, without the ability to set or modify product prices. | MUST HAVE | • Vendor can update product descriptions and images
• Vendor can update inventory quantities
• Vendor portal does not expose any pricing fields | --- ### Inventory & SAP ERP Integration | FR-ID | Requirement (The system SHALL...) | Priority | Acceptance Criteria | |-------|-----------------------------------|----------|---------------------| | FR-027 | sync inventory levels with the client's SAP ERP system in real time (≤5 minutes), automatically marking products with zero inventory as out of stock and re-listing them when stock is restored. | MUST HAVE | • Inventory levels are pulled from SAP and reflected on the platform within ≤5 minutes
• Products with zero inventory are automatically marked as out of stock and cannot be added to cart
• Products returning to stock are automatically re-listed as available | | FR-028 | sync product information from the client's SAP ERP system via a daily batch process, supporting both real-time and batch sync modes, with failures logged and alerted. | MUST HAVE | • Product information is synced from SAP via daily batch process
• Both real-time and batch sync modes are supported
• Sync failures are logged and an alert is triggered | | FR-029 | support migration of approximately 8,000 existing product SKUs from the client's SAP ERP system prior to go-live, with validation of completeness and accuracy. | MUST HAVE | • All existing SAP product records are successfully imported into the platform before go-live
• Migrated product data is validated for completeness and accuracy
• Migration results report is produced identifying any failed or incomplete records | --- ### Logistics Integration | FR-ID | Requirement (The system SHALL...) | Priority | Acceptance Criteria | |-------|-----------------------------------|----------|---------------------| | FR-030 | integrate with the Delhivery and BlueDart logistics APIs to retrieve real-time shipping rates at checkout and enable real-time order tracking from within the platform. | MUST HAVE | • Shipping rate from Delhivery and BlueDart is displayed at checkout
• Customer can track their shipment status in real time from the platform
• Tracking data is fetched directly from the logistics partner API | --- ### Reporting & Analytics | FR-ID | Requirement (The system SHALL...) | Priority | Acceptance Criteria | |-------|-----------------------------------|----------|---------------------| | FR-031 | provide central admins with sales reports filterable by daily, weekly, and monthly time periods, segmented by region, product category, and vendor, with export capability. | MUST HAVE | • Admin can generate a sales report filtered by date range (daily/weekly/monthly)
• Report can be segmented by region, category, and vendor
• Report data is exportable | | FR-032 | provide central admins with a cart abandonment analytics report showing products added to cart but not purchased, including add-to-cart count, purchase count, and abandonment rate, filterable by date range, category, and region. | SHOULD HAVE | • Admin can view a cart abandonment report listing products added to cart but not purchased
• Report shows product name, number of times added to cart, number of times purchased, and abandonment rate
• Report can be filtered by date range, category, and region | --- ### Marketing & Promotions | FR-ID | Requirement (The system SHALL...) | Priority | Acceptance Criteria | |-------|-----------------------------------|----------|---------------------| | FR-033 | allow central admins to create, schedule, and send promotional campaigns to targeted customer segments via email, SMS, or both simultaneously, with customer opt-out capability. | SHOULD HAVE | • Admin can create a campaign with a subject, message body, and target audience segment
• Admin can schedule a campaign for immediate or future delivery
• Campaign can be sent via email, SMS, or both simultaneously
• Customers can opt out of marketing communications | --- ### Mobile Application | FR-ID | Requirement (The system SHALL...) | Priority | Acceptance Criteria | |-------|-----------------------------------|----------|---------------------| | FR-034 | provide a fully functional native or responsive mobile application for Android and iOS, supporting the two latest major versions of each OS, available on the Google Play Store and Apple App Store. | MUST HAVE | • The ShopEase application is available for download on the Google Play Store (Android) and Apple App Store (iOS)
• All core customer-facing features (browse, search, cart, checkout, order tracking) are available on the mobile app
• The mobile app supports the two latest major versions of Android and iOS | | FR-035 | provide an executive mobile dashboard displaying key business metrics — including total revenue, number of orders, and active users — accessible via role-based access control and optimized for mobile screens. | SHOULD HAVE | • Executive dashboard is accessible via the mobile app with role-based access control
• Dashboard displays total revenue, number of orders, and active users for configurable time periods
• Metrics are presented in a visually clear format (charts, KPI cards) optimized for mobile screens |

sections.name: Business Rules
sections.content:
## Business Rules

| BR-ID | Rule Statement | Module | Priority |
|-------|---------------|--------|----------|
| BR-001 | The system must present an EMI payment option at checkout for all orders meeting the eligible order value threshold. | Checkout / Payments | MUST_HAVE |
| BR-002 | No-Cost EMI financing options must be offered exclusively through Bajaj Finance as the confirmed financing partner. | Checkout / Payments | MUST_HAVE |
| BR-003 | All product pricing on the ShopEase platform must be set and modified exclusively by the Central Admin Team. | Product Management | MUST_HAVE |
| BR-004 | The system must reject any price modification request submitted by a Vendor Partner role. | Product Management | MUST_HAVE |
| BR-005 | Inventory availability must be synchronised with SAP ERP in real time to ensure out-of-stock products are never displayed as available. | Inventory / SAP Integration | MUST_HAVE |
| BR-006 | Product information updates originating from SAP ERP must be reflected on the ShopEase platform within 24 hours via daily batch sync. | Inventory / SAP Integration | MUST_HAVE |
| BR-007 | A customer-submitted product review must not be published or visible to any other customer until explicitly approved by an admin moderator. | Review Moderation Workflow | MUST_HAVE |
| BR-008 | The system must maintain a moderation queue that allows admin moderators to review, approve, or reject each submitted customer review. | Review Moderation Workflow | MUST_HAVE |

sections.name: User Roles and Permissions
sections.content:
# User Roles and Permissions

**Project:** ShopEase-2
**Client:** RetailCorp India Pvt. Ltd.
**Prepared by:** Arjun Shah, Business Analyst
**Version:** 1.0

---

## User Roles and Permissions

### Role Definitions

**End Customer**
The End Customer is a registered or guest user who browses the ShopEase catalog, places orders, and manages their personal account. This role has access exclusively to customer-facing features including product search, cart, checkout, order tracking, wishlist, reviews, and return requests.

**Central Admin**
The Central Admin belongs to RetailCorp's internal Central Admin Team and holds the highest level of administrative access across the platform. This role is responsible for managing the full product catalog, pricing, promotions, user accounts, review moderation, and all reporting and analytics functions.

**Store Manager**
The Store Manager is a regional user assigned to a specific physical RetailCorp store location, with access scoped strictly to orders and return requests originating from their assigned region. This role cannot access platform-wide data, pricing controls, or vendor management functions.

**Vendor Partner**
The Vendor Partner is a third-party seller who accesses a dedicated vendor portal to manage their own product listings and inventory quantities. Per TR-013, Vendor Partners are explicitly restricted from viewing or modifying any product pricing fields, and their data access is limited to their own catalog and stock information only.

---

### Permissions Matrix

| # | Feature | End Customer | Store Manager | Central Admin | Vendor Partner |
|---|---------|:------------:|:-------------:|:-------------:|:--------------:|
| 1 | Customer Registration & Login (Email / OTP) | ✓ Allowed | ✗ Denied | ✗ Denied | ✗ Denied |
| 2 | Product Search (by Name, Brand, Category) | ✓ Allowed | ✗ Denied | ✓ Allowed | Limited (own listings only) |
| 3 | Product Filtering (Price, Brand, Rating, Delivery Time) | ✓ Allowed | ✗ Denied | ✓ Allowed | ✗ Denied |
| 4 | View Product Detail Page (Images, Description, Reviews) | ✓ Allowed | ✗ Denied | ✓ Allowed | Limited (own listings only) |
| 5 | Shopping Cart Management (Add, Update, Remove Items) | ✓ Allowed | ✗ Denied | ✗ Denied | ✗ Denied |
| 6 | Checkout & Payment (Credit/Debit Card, UPI, Net Banking, EMI) | ✓ Allowed | ✗ Denied | ✗ Denied | ✗ Denied |
| 7 | Wishlist Management (Add, View, Remove Products) | ✓ Allowed | ✗ Denied | ✗ Denied | ✗ Denied |
| 8 | Delivery Address Management (Add, Save, Select) | ✓ Allowed | ✗ Denied | ✗ Denied | ✗ Denied |
| 9 | Real-Time Order Tracking (via Delhivery / BlueDart APIs) | ✓ Allowed | Limited (regional orders only) | ✓ Allowed | ✗ Denied |
| 10 | Submit Product Review & Star Rating | ✓ Allowed (delivered orders only) | ✗ Denied | ✗ Denied | ✗ Denied |
| 11 | Initiate Return Request | ✓ Allowed (own orders only) | ✗ Denied | ✗ Denied | ✗ Denied |
| 12 | View & Filter Regional Orders | ✗ Denied | ✓ Allowed (assigned region only) | ✓ Allowed (all regions) | ✗ Denied |
| 13 | Approve / Manage Return Requests | ✗ Denied | ✓ Allowed (regional scope) | ✓ Allowed (all regions) | ✗ Denied |
| 14 | Product Catalog Management (Add, Edit, Deactivate Products) | ✗ Denied | ✗ Denied | ✓ Allowed | Limited (own listings only) |
| 15 | Product Pricing & Discount Configuration | ✗ Denied | ✗ Denied | ✓ Allowed | ✗ Denied |
| 16 | Inventory Management & SAP ERP Sync | ✗ Denied | ✗ Denied | ✓ Allowed | Limited (own stock quantities only) |
| 17 | Review Moderation (Approve / Reject Pending Reviews) | ✗ Denied | ✗ Denied | ✓ Allowed | ✗ Denied |
| 18 | Sales Reports (Daily / Weekly / Monthly by Region, Category, Vendor) | ✗ Denied | Limited (regional sales only) | ✓ Allowed | Limited (own sales data only) |
| 19 | Cart Abandonment Analytics | ✗ Denied | ✗ Denied | ✓ Allowed | ✗ Denied |
| 20 | Promotional Campaign Management (Email & SMS) | ✗ Denied | ✗ Denied | ✓ Allowed | ✗ Denied |
| 21 | Executive Mobile Dashboard (Revenue, Orders, Active Users) | ✗ Denied | ✗ Denied | ✓ Allowed | ✗ Denied |
| 22 | User Account & Role Management | ✗ Denied | ✗ Denied | ✓ Allowed | ✗ Denied |

---

**Legend:**
- **✓ Allowed** — Full access to the feature with no restrictions
- **✗ Denied** — No access; feature is not visible or accessible to this role
- **Limited** — Partial access; scope or actions are restricted as described in the cell

> **Note on Data Residency:** All data accessed through the above permissions is subject to the Data Residency requirements applicable to RetailCorp India Pvt. Ltd. No role is permitted to export or transmit customer data outside compliant storage boundaries.

> **Note on Role Isolation:** Per TR-011, each role accesses the platform through a separate login portal with role-specific credentials. No cross-role access or privilege escalation is supported by default.

sections.name: User Journeys and Use Cases
sections.content:
# ShopEase-2 User Journeys | RetailCorp | E-Commerce

---

### UC-001: Customer Product Discovery to Cart

**Actor**: End Customer
**Goal**: Search, filter, and add a desired product to cart

**Steps**:
1. Customer logs in via email/password or OTP (US-002)
2. Enters product name, brand, or category in the search bar (US-003, TR-018)
3. Applies filters: price range, brand, rating, delivery time (US-004, TR-019)
4. Selects a product and reviews the detail page — images, specs, reviews, availability (US-005)
5. Confirms stock availability synced from SAP ERP in real time (US-015, TR-016)
6. Adds product to cart; cart displays name, quantity, unit price, and subtotal (US-006)

**Outcome**: Product is successfully added to cart with accurate pricing and live inventory status confirmed.

---

### UC-002: Customer Checkout and Payment

**Actor**: End Customer
**Goal**: Complete a purchase using a preferred payment method including EMI

**Steps**:
1. Customer reviews cart contents and proceeds to checkout (US-006)
2. Selects or adds a delivery address with full address fields (US-008)
3. Platform fetches real-time shipping rates from Delhivery and BlueDart APIs; customer selects preferred carrier (US-024, TR-022)
4. Customer selects payment method: credit card, debit card, UPI, net banking, or EMI via Bajaj Finance (US-009, TR-004, TR-005)
5. EMI tenure and interest details are displayed before confirmation (TR-005)
6. Payment is processed; order confirmation is displayed with reference number (US-009)

**Outcome**: Order is placed successfully with shipment created via logistics API and payment confirmed.

---

### UC-003: Customer Order Tracking and Return Request

**Actor**: End Customer
**Goal**: Track a dispatched order and initiate a return if required

**Steps**:
1. Customer navigates to order history from their account dashboard (US-010)
2. Selects an active order; tracking page displays live shipment status fetched from Delhivery or BlueDart API (US-010, TR-023)
3. Status updates — confirmed, packed, shipped, out for delivery, delivered — without page refresh (US-010)
4. Upon delivery, customer identifies an issue and navigates to the delivered order (US-023)
5. Initiates return request, selects reason: defective, not as described, or wrong item (US-023)
6. System generates return confirmation with a unique reference number (US-023)

**Outcome**: Customer successfully tracks delivery in real time and raises a return request with confirmed reference.

---

### UC-004: Vendor Product Listing Management

**Actor**: Vendor Partner
**Goal**: Add and update product listings and inventory quantities via the vendor portal

**Steps**:
1. Vendor logs in to the dedicated vendor portal using role-specific credentials (TR-011, US-016)
2. Vendor portal displays only listing and inventory management features; no pricing fields are exposed (TR-013)
3. Vendor adds a new product listing: name, description, images, and stock quantity (US-016, TR-013)
4. Vendor updates inventory quantities for existing SKUs (TR-013)
5. Vendor edits product description or images for an existing listing (TR-013)
6. Changes are saved and reflected on the platform pending admin pricing and activation (US-013)

**Outcome**: Vendor successfully manages product listings and inventory without access to any pricing configuration.

---

### UC-005: Central Admin Sales Reporting and Cart Abandonment Analysis

**Actor**: Central Admin
**Goal**: Generate sales reports and review cart abandonment data to support business decisions

**Steps**:
1. Central Admin logs in via the admin portal with role-specific credentials (TR-011)
2. Navigates to the sales reporting module; selects date range: daily, weekly, or monthly (US-018, TR-028)
3. Applies segment filters: region, product category, and vendor (US-018, TR-028)
4. Exports the filtered sales report for offline analysis (TR-028)
5. Navigates to the cart abandonment analytics dashboard (US-019, TR-029)
6. Reviews abandonment rate per product — cart additions vs. purchases — filtered by date range and category (US-019)

**Outcome**: Admin obtains actionable sales and abandonment insights segmented by region, category, and vendor for decision-making.

sections.name: Data Requirements
sections.content:
## Data Requirements

### Key Data Entities

| Entity | Key Attributes | Estimated Volume |
|---|---|---|
| Customer Account | Customer ID, name, email, mobile, hashed password, registration date, opt-out preferences, account status | TBD at discovery |
| Product / SKU | SKU ID, name, brand, category, description, images, base price, discount config, availability status, vendor ID | 8,000 at go-live; scalable to 25,000 |
| Inventory | SKU ID, stock quantity, warehouse location, last SAP sync timestamp, sync status | Per SKU; real-time sync |
| Order | Order ID, customer ID, SKU(s), quantity, pricing snapshot, payment method, EMI details, carrier, tracking ID, status, region | Peaks at 50,000 concurrent sessions |
| Payment Transaction | Transaction ID, order ID, payment method, gateway reference, PCI-DSS tokenised card data, EMI tenure, financing partner, status | Per order |
| User Role & Access | User ID, role (customer/store manager/vendor/central admin), assigned region, portal access flags, login credentials | TBD |
| Delivery Address | Address ID, customer ID, name, street, city, state, PIN code, phone, default flag | Multiple per customer |
| Product Review | Review ID, customer ID, SKU ID, star rating, review text, submission date, moderation status, moderator ID | Per delivered order |
| Wishlist | Wishlist ID, customer ID, SKU ID(s), date added | Per registered customer |
| Cart Abandonment Event | Session ID, customer ID, SKU ID, add-to-cart timestamp, purchase flag | High volume during sale events |
| Campaign / Communication | Campaign ID, type (email/SMS), target segment, content, schedule, opt-out compliance flag | Per admin-created campaign |
| SAP Sync Log | Sync ID, entity type, sync mode (real-time/batch), timestamp, status, error details | Per sync event |

### Data Migration

Approximately 8,000 SKUs will be migrated from RetailCorp's SAP ERP system prior to go-live, including product names, descriptions, categories, brands, pricing, and inventory levels. A validation process must confirm completeness and accuracy of all migrated records before the platform is made live. Any SAP records failing validation must be flagged, reported, and resolved before cutover.

### Data Retention

Customer personal data must comply with GDPR right-to-erasure requirements and the Indian IT Act, with a defined deletion workflow for opt-out requests processed within 24 hours. Order and payment transaction records must be retained for a minimum period aligned with Indian financial and tax regulations. Audit logs, SAP sync logs, and moderation records must be retained for a minimum of 12 months to support operational and compliance reviews.

sections.name: Integration Requirements
sections.content:
# Integration Requirements
## ShopEase-2 | RetailCorp | E-Commerce

---

### SAP ERP

| Attribute | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Type** | System-to-System API Integration + Batch File Transfer |
| **Direction** | Bidirectional — SAP ERP → ShopEase (inventory levels, product data); ShopEase → SAP ERP (order confirmations, return requests) |
| **Data Exchanged** | Inbound to ShopEase: Product catalog records (name, brand, category, description, specifications, SKU, price), real-time inventory levels, stock availability updates. Outbound from ShopEase: Order status updates, return request data. Migration (one-time): Approximately 8,000 existing SKU records for pre-go-live data migration. |
| **Frequency** | Real-time sync for inventory levels (≤5 minutes latency per US-015); Daily batch process for product information updates (TR-016); One-time bulk migration of ~8,000 SKUs prior to go-live (TR-031) |
| **Owner** | RetailCorp Central Admin Team (business owner); ShopEase integration team (technical owner) |
| **Fallback** | Sync failures must be logged and alerted to the admin team (TR-016 AC). Platform to display last known inventory state with a staleness indicator. Products must not be listed as available if stock cannot be confirmed. Manual override capability required for Central Admin. |
| **Status** | Confirmed — existing commercial system at RetailCorp; integration is mandatory for go-live (MUST_HAVE, TR-016, TR-031, US-015) |

---

### Bajaj Finance

| Attribute | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Type** | Third-Party REST API Integration (Financing / Lending Partner) |
| **Direction** | Bidirectional — ShopEase → Bajaj Finance (EMI eligibility request, loan initiation, customer details); Bajaj Finance → ShopEase (EMI plan options, tenure details, approval status, transaction confirmation) |
| **Data Exchanged** | Outbound from ShopEase: Customer identifier, order value, product details, selected EMI tenure. Inbound to ShopEase: Available no-cost EMI plans, tenure options, interest details (zero-interest confirmation), approval or rejection status, transaction reference ID. |
| **Frequency** | Real-time — triggered at checkout when customer selects no-cost EMI as payment method |
| **Owner** | RetailCorp (commercial agreement owner); ShopEase payments integration team (technical owner) |
| **Fallback** | If Bajaj Finance API is unavailable, no-cost EMI option via Bajaj Finance must be hidden or disabled at checkout with an appropriate customer-facing message. Other payment methods must remain fully functional. Fallback state must be logged. |
| **Status** | Confirmed — existing commercial agreement in place with Bajaj Finance (TR-005, US-009, MUST_HAVE) |

---

### ZestMoney

| Attribute | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Type** | Third-Party REST API Integration (Financing / Lending Partner) |
| **Direction** | Bidirectional — ShopEase → ZestMoney (EMI eligibility request, loan initiation, customer details); ZestMoney → ShopEase (EMI plan options, tenure details, approval or rejection status, transaction confirmation) |
| **Data Exchanged** | Outbound from ShopEase: Customer identifier, order value, product details, selected EMI tenure. Inbound to ShopEase: Available EMI plans, tenure and interest details, approval or rejection status, transaction reference ID. |
| **Frequency** | Real-time — triggered at checkout when customer selects ZestMoney EMI as payment method |
| **Owner** | RetailCorp (commercial relationship owner); ShopEase payments integration team (technical owner) |
| **Fallback** | If ZestMoney API is unavailable, ZestMoney EMI option must be hidden or disabled at checkout with an appropriate customer-facing message. Bajaj Finance and other payment methods must remain unaffected. Fallback state must be logged. |
| **Status** | Under consideration — referenced in TR-005 as a financing partner being evaluated; commercial agreement not yet confirmed. Integration design should be included but activation is conditional on agreement finalization. |

---

### Delhivery

| Attribute | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Type** | Third-Party REST API Integration (Logistics Provider) |
| **Direction** | Bidirectional — ShopEase → Delhivery (shipment creation request, order details, delivery address, package dimensions and weight); Delhivery → ShopEase (real-time shipping rates, shipment tracking status, delivery checkpoints, estimated delivery date and time) |
| **Data Exchanged** | Outbound from ShopEase: Delivery address (street, city, state, PIN code), order weight and dimensions, selected service level. Inbound to ShopEase: Shipping rate quotes, shipment tracking ID, real-time tracking status (confirmed, packed, shipped, out for delivery, delivered), last known checkpoint, estimated delivery date and time. |
| **Frequency** | Real-time — shipping rate fetch triggered at checkout; shipment creation triggered upon order confirmation; tracking status polled at a defined interval or via webhook to support live tracking without page refresh (TR-023, US-010) |
| **Owner** | RetailCorp (commercial relationship owner); ShopEase logistics integration team (technical owner) |
| **Fallback** | If Delhivery API is unavailable at checkout, Delhivery shipping option must be hidden and BlueDart must remain available. If tracking API is unavailable, last known status must be displayed with a timestamp. All API failures must be logged and alerted. Shipment creation failures must trigger an immediate alert to the operations team. |
| **Status** | Confirmed — explicitly named as a mandatory logistics integration (MUST_HAVE, TR-022, US-024) |

---

### BlueDart

| Attribute | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Type** | Third-Party REST API Integration (Logistics Provider) |
| **Direction** | Bidirectional — ShopEase → BlueDart (shipment creation request, order details, delivery address, package dimensions and weight); BlueDart → ShopEase (real-time shipping rates, shipment tracking status, delivery checkpoints, estimated delivery date and time) |
| **Data Exchanged** | Outbound from ShopEase: Delivery address (street, city, state, PIN code), order weight and dimensions, selected service level. Inbound to ShopEase: Shipping rate quotes, shipment tracking ID, real-time tracking status (confirmed, packed, shipped, out for delivery, delivered), last known checkpoint, estimated delivery date and time. |
| **Frequency** | Real-time — shipping rate fetch triggered at checkout; shipment creation triggered upon order confirmation; tracking status polled at a defined interval or via webhook to support live tracking without page refresh (TR-023, US-010) |
| **Owner** | RetailCorp (commercial relationship owner); ShopEase logistics integration team (technical owner) |
| **Fallback** | If BlueDart API is unavailable at checkout, BlueDart shipping option must be hidden and Delhivery must remain available. If tracking API is unavailable, last known status must be displayed with a timestamp. All API failures must be logged and alerted. Shipment creation failures must trigger an immediate alert to the operations team. |
| **Status** | Confirmed — explicitly named as a mandatory logistics integration (MUST_HAVE, TR-022, US-024) |

---

### Third-Party Marketing Platform

| Attribute | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Type** | Third-Party API Integration (Email and SMS Campaign Delivery) |
| **Direction** | Outbound — ShopEase → Marketing Platform (campaign content, target customer segment, scheduled delivery time, opt-out list); Marketing Platform → ShopEase (delivery status, opt-out updates, campaign performance metrics) |
| **Data Exchanged** | Outbound from ShopEase: Customer contact list (email addresses, mobile numbers), audience segment definition, campaign subject and message body, scheduled send time, opt-out suppression list. Inbound to ShopEase: Delivery confirmation, bounce and failure reports, opt-out registrations to be synced back to customer profiles. |
| **Frequency** | On-demand — triggered when Central Admin creates and schedules a promotional campaign (TR-030, US-020); opt-out sync should be near real-time to ensure compliance |
| **Owner** | RetailCorp Central Admin Team (business owner); ShopEase marketing integration team (technical owner) |
| **Fallback** | If the third-party marketing platform API is unavailable, campaign scheduling must queue the request and retry. Admins must be notified of delivery failures. Customer opt-out requests must be honoured immediately within the ShopEase platform regardless of third-party sync status. |
| **Status** | Conditional — TR-030 states the platform must support promotional email and SMS either via a built-in tool or third-party integration (SHOULD_HAVE). Specific platform vendor not yet confirmed. Integration design required; vendor selection pending. |

sections.name: Assumptions
sections.content:
## Assumptions

| A-ID | Assumption Statement | Risk if Wrong | Owner |
|------|----------------------|---------------|-------|
| A-001 | RetailCorp India is a mid-size retail chain with approximately 200 physical stores across India, and ShopEase-2 represents a greenfield online channel with no existing e-commerce infrastructure to migrate or integrate. | Scope, architecture, and effort estimates may be significantly impacted if legacy online systems or data exist and require migration or integration. | Sunita Agarwal (RetailCorp) |
| A-002 | A signed commercial agreement between RetailCorp India and Bajaj Finance is already in place, enabling No-Cost EMI integration to proceed without further contractual delays. | Bajaj Finance integration timeline will be delayed, impacting the payment options available at launch. | Rajesh Mehta (RetailCorp) |
| A-003 | A commercial agreement with ZestMoney has not yet been finalized, and ZestMoney integration is therefore not confirmed for the initial launch scope. | If the agreement is assumed finalized prematurely, development effort may be committed to an integration that cannot go live, wasting sprint capacity. | Rajesh Mehta (RetailCorp) |
| A-004 | The current SAP ERP product catalog contains approximately 8,000 SKUs at project initiation, and will scale to approximately 25,000 SKUs upon completion of vendor onboarding. | Platform architecture, catalog management design, and performance benchmarks may be under-engineered if actual SKU volumes are significantly higher or growth occurs faster than anticipated. | Rajesh Mehta (RetailCorp) |

sections.name: Constraints
sections.content:
## Constraints

| C-ID | Constraint | Category | Impact |
|------|------------|----------|--------|
| C-001 | The ShopEase platform must be fully deployed and accessible to end users within 6 months of project kickoff, as mandated by the RetailCorp CEO. | Timeline | All design, development, testing, and deployment activities must be planned and executed within a fixed 6-month window, leaving no buffer for scope expansion or major rework. |
| C-002 | All customer personally identifiable information (PII) must be stored and processed exclusively on servers physically located within India, with no cross-border data transfer permitted. | Regulatory | Cloud architecture, third-party integrations (including Delhivery, BlueDart, ZestMoney, and Bajaj Finance), and any analytics pipelines must be audited and confirmed to comply with India-only data residency requirements before go-live. |
| C-003 | The cloud infrastructure provider selected for ShopEase must have an active, production-grade data center presence within India to satisfy RetailCorp's data residency obligations. | Technical | The choice of cloud provider is restricted to vendors offering India-region availability zones, limiting infrastructure options and potentially affecting cost, service features, and vendor negotiation flexibility. |

sections.name: Dependencies
sections.content:
## Dependencies

| # | Dependency | Owner | Required By | Impact if Delayed |
|---|------------|-------|-------------|-------------------|
| 1 | **Bajaj Finance Commercial Agreement Confirmation** — The existing commercial agreement with Bajaj Finance must be formally validated and shared with the development team before integration design can begin. API credentials, sandbox access, and integration documentation must be provided by Bajaj Finance. | Rajesh Mehta (RetailCorp) / Bajaj Finance Partnership Team | Integration design sprint; required before development of No-Cost EMI payment flow | Bajaj Finance EMI option cannot be built or tested; payment feature scope is reduced at launch, directly impacting customer checkout experience |
| 2 | **ZestMoney Commercial Agreement Finalization** — A pending commercial agreement with ZestMoney must be signed before ZestMoney integration can be scoped, designed, or developed. Until the agreement is executed, ZestMoney remains out of confirmed scope. | Rajesh Mehta (RetailCorp) / ZestMoney Business Team | Post-launch delivery planning; must be resolved before any ZestMoney development sprint is scheduled | ZestMoney integration cannot proceed; feature will be deferred to post-launch release, reducing No-Cost EMI provider options available to customers at go-live |
| 3 | **SAP ERP API Access and Integration Readiness** — The existing SAP ERP system used by RetailCorp must expose stable APIs or data export mechanisms for product catalog synchronization, inventory levels, and order data. SAP ERP access credentials, data schema documentation, and a sandbox/test environment must be provided to the ShopEase development team. | RetailCorp Central Admin Team / SAP ERP System Owner | Catalog import design and development; required before SKU migration of ~8,000 initial SKUs can begin | Product catalog cannot be seeded into ShopEase; inventory data will be unavailable; platform cannot go live without accurate product and stock data synced from SAP ERP |
| 4 | **Initial Product Catalog Data Export from SAP ERP (~8,000 SKUs)** — A clean, validated export of the current ~8,000 SKU catalog from SAP ERP must be prepared, reviewed for data quality, and delivered to the ShopEase team for import and mapping. | RetailCorp Central Admin Team / Catalog Operations Team | Catalog build and UAT phase; required well ahead of the 6-month go-live deadline (TR-001) | Platform launches with incomplete or no product catalog; customers cannot browse or purchase; go-live date (TR-001) is directly at risk |
| 5 | **Vendor Onboarding Process and Vendor Partner Data Readiness** — The vendor onboarding workflow must be defined and executed to enable the catalog to scale from ~8,000 to ~25,000 SKUs post-launch. Vendor Partner agreements, product data templates, and onboarding timelines must be established before the scaled catalog can be populated. | RetailCorp Central Admin Team / Vendor Partner Management Team | Post-launch catalog scaling; vendor onboarding design must be completed before launch | Catalog remains at ~8,000 SKUs; platform growth targets are not met; Vendor Partner portal features cannot be validated without active vendor participants |
| 6 | **Delhivery API Integration Credentials and Documentation** — Delhivery must provide API credentials, sandbox environment access, and technical integration documentation (shipping rate calculation, order dispatch, tracking) to the ShopEase development team before logistics integration can begin. | RetailCorp Logistics Owner / Delhivery Partner Team | Logistics and order fulfillment module development; required before end-to-end order flow can be tested | Shipping rate calculation, order dispatch, and delivery tracking features cannot be built or tested; order fulfillment flow is incomplete at launch |
| 7 | **BlueDart API Integration Credentials and Documentation** — BlueDart must provide API credentials, sandbox environment access, and technical integration documentation to the ShopEase development team. BlueDart serves as the second logistics provider, and its integration is required for full carrier coverage. | RetailCorp Logistics Owner / BlueDart Partner Team | Logistics module development; required in parallel with Delhivery integration | Carrier redundancy is unavailable at launch; orders cannot be routed to BlueDart; fulfillment reliability is reduced, particularly for regions where BlueDart is the preferred carrier |
| 8 | **India-Region Cloud Infrastructure Provisioning** — A cloud infrastructure provider with a confirmed data center presence in India must be selected, contracted, and provisioned before any production environment setup can begin. This is required to satisfy the data residency constraint (TR-025, TR-040). | RetailCorp IT / Infrastructure Owner | Infrastructure setup; must be completed before any production deployment activity begins | Platform cannot be deployed in a compliant environment; data residency requirements (TR-025, TR-040) are violated; go-live is blocked until compliant infrastructure is available |
| 9 | **Cloud Provider Data Residency Compliance Confirmation** — The selected cloud provider must formally confirm that all provisioned services — including databases, object storage, and compute — are restricted to Indian data center regions. Written confirmation or compliance documentation must be obtained before production data is loaded. | RetailCorp IT / Cloud Provider Account Team | Pre-production environment sign-off; required before any customer PII is stored in the system | Customer data may be stored or processed outside India, violating TR-025 and TR-040; regulatory and contractual breach risk; platform cannot be certified for go-live |
| 10 | **CEO-Mandated 6-Month Go-Live Deadline Governance** — All upstream dependencies (SAP ERP access, cloud provisioning, Bajaj Finance integration, logistics API access, catalog data) must be tracked against the hard 6-month deadline set by the RetailCorp CEO (TR-001). A dependency resolution schedule must be agreed upon at project kickoff to ensure no single dependency causes a deadline breach. | Sunita Agarwal (RetailCorp CEO Sponsor) / Project Manager | Project kickoff; dependency resolution schedule must be baselined in week 1 | Any unresolved dependency cascades into a go-live delay; the CEO-mandated deadline (TR-001) is breached, with associated contractual, reputational, and commercial consequences for RetailCorp |

sections.name: Risks
sections.content:
## Risks

| Risk ID | Risk | Probability | Impact | Level | Mitigation |
|---------|------|-------------|--------|-------|------------|
| RSK-001 | The 6-month hard deadline (TR-001) may be missed if discovery, development, and UAT phases are not tightly sequenced, given the greenfield nature of the platform (TR-002) and the breadth of integrations required (SAP ERP, Delhivery, BlueDart, Bajaj Finance) | High | High | High | Establish a locked delivery roadmap at project kickoff with milestone gates; prioritize MVP scope; assign a dedicated project manager to track velocity weekly; escalate blockers to steering committee within 48 hours |
| RSK-002 | ZestMoney commercial agreement (TR-006, TR-033) may not be finalized before or during the build phase, causing scope ambiguity, rework of the payment module, or a post-launch integration gap that affects customer financing options | High | Medium | High | Treat ZestMoney as a conditionally scoped module; design the payment integration layer to be provider-agnostic; set a contractual decision deadline with RetailCorp no later than Month 2; document fallback to Bajaj Finance-only launch |
| RSK-003 | SAP ERP integration for catalog sync may be delayed or unstable if the existing SAP instance has data quality issues, non-standard configurations, or limited API availability, risking incomplete or incorrect product data at launch | High | High | High | Conduct a SAP integration spike in Month 1 to validate API availability and data quality; define a data cleansing plan for the existing 8,000 SKUs; establish a dedicated SAP liaison from RetailCorp's IT team throughout the project |
| RSK-004 | Catalog migration and vendor onboarding scaling from 8,000 to 25,000 SKUs (TR-015) may introduce performance bottlenecks, data inconsistencies, or delayed go-live readiness if onboarding tooling and processes are not built and tested early | Medium | High | High | Design the catalog and SKU management system for 25,000+ SKUs from the outset; conduct load testing at projected scale before launch; build a self-service vendor onboarding portal with validation rules to reduce manual effort |
