| RSK-005 | Data residency compliance (TR-025, TR-040) may be breached if any third-party service, CDN, analytics tool, or logistics API inadvertently transmits or stores customer PII outside India, exposing RetailCorp to regulatory and reputational risk | Medium | High | High | Conduct a data flow audit for every third-party integration before go-live; enforce contractual data residency clauses with all vendors; configure cloud infrastructure exclusively in India regions; include data residency in the security sign-off checklist |
| RSK-006 | Delhivery and BlueDart API integrations may face instability, rate limiting, or breaking changes during the build phase, causing order fulfillment and shipment tracking features to be incomplete or unreliable at launch | Medium | High | High | Build an abstraction layer over logistics APIs to isolate platform code from provider-specific changes; establish sandbox access and SLA commitments with both providers in Month 1; implement retry logic and fallback alerting for API failures |
| RSK-007 | RetailCorp's Central Admin Team and Store Managers may have limited digital platform experience given the greenfield online channel context (TR-002), leading to low adoption, misconfiguration of the platform, or delayed UAT sign-off | Medium | High | High | Schedule role-based training sessions for Central Admin and Store Manager personas before UAT; develop user guides and in-platform help content; include key business users in iterative review cycles from Month 2 onward |
| RSK-008 | The assumption that Bajaj Finance integration can proceed immediately (TR-006) may prove incorrect if technical documentation, sandbox credentials, or API access from Bajaj Finance are delayed, compressing the payment integration timeline | Medium | Medium | Medium | Initiate Bajaj Finance technical onboarding in Week 1 of the project; obtain sandbox credentials and API documentation before development begins; identify an alternative No-Cost EMI delivery approach if integration is delayed beyond Month 3 |
| RSK-009 | Key requirement sources (Sunita Agarwal, Rajesh Mehta) represent a narrow set of stakeholders; undocumented requirements from Store Managers, Vendor Partners, or the Central Admin Team may surface late in the project, causing scope creep and timeline risk | Medium | High | High | Conduct structured discovery workshops with Store Manager and Vendor Partner representatives in Month 1; establish a formal change control process with impact assessment for any post-baseline scope additions; freeze scope by end of Month 2 |
| RSK-010 | Cloud infrastructure provisioning in an India-region data center (TR-040) may face capacity constraints, longer-than-expected provisioning timelines, or cost overruns if the selected cloud provider has limited India-region availability for required services | Low | High | Medium | Confirm India-region service availability and capacity with the chosen cloud provider before contract signing; provision infrastructure no later than Month 1; include infrastructure costs in the project budget baseline with a contingency buffer |

sections.name: Glossary
sections.content:
## Glossary

| Term | Definition |
|---|---|
| Bajaj Finance | A confirmed financing partner with an existing commercial agreement in place, integrated into the ShopEase platform to provide no-cost EMI payment options to customers at checkout. |
| BlueDart | A second third-party logistics provider whose API will be integrated into the ShopEase platform to provide real-time shipping rate calculation and order tracking services. |
| Cart Abandonment Analytics | A reporting feature that tracks products customers viewed or added to their shopping cart but did not proceed to purchase, enabling admins to identify and address conversion issues. |
| Central Admin Team | RetailCorp's internal team with the highest level of administrative access on the ShopEase platform, responsible for managing all products, pricing, promotions, and content across the platform. |
| Data Residency | The regulatory and contractual requirement that all customer data collected and processed by the ShopEase platform must be stored exclusively on servers physically located within India. |
| Delhivery | A third-party logistics provider whose API will be integrated into the ShopEase platform to provide real-time shipping rate calculation and order tracking services. |
| No-Cost EMI | An equated monthly installment payment option where the customer pays the product price in fixed monthly installments with zero interest, facilitated through financing partners such as Bajaj Finance. |
| RetailCorp India | The client organization, a mid-size retail chain with approximately 200 physical stores across India, for whom the ShopEase e-commerce platform is being built as a greenfield online channel. |
| Review Moderation Workflow | The process by which customer-submitted product reviews are held in a pending state and must be explicitly approved or rejected by a central admin moderator before becoming visible to other customers on the platform. |
| SAP ERP | The client's existing enterprise resource planning system used to manage product data and inventory, which will be integrated with the ShopEase platform to support both real-time inventory sync and daily batch product data sync. |
| ShopEase | The e-commerce platform being built for RetailCorp India Pvt. Ltd., serving as the organization's greenfield online retail channel for customers across India. |
| SKU | Stock Keeping Unit — a unique identifier assigned to each distinct product variant in the catalog, used to track inventory and product information across the ShopEase platform and SAP ERP system. |
| Store Manager | A regional user role assigned to managers of individual physical RetailCorp stores, with platform access scoped to viewing orders and managing return requests within their assigned region. |
| Vendor Partner | A third-party seller who lists products on the ShopEase platform and is provided a dedicated login portal to manage their product listings and inventory, without the ability to set or modify pricing. |
| ZestMoney | A financing partner being considered for no-cost EMI integration on the ShopEase platform, whose inclusion in scope is contingent on the finalization of a pending commercial agreement with RetailCorp. |

sections.content:

7. Delhivery API Integration Specification

### Appendix B: Open Questions and Action Items

- **[OQ-001]** Confirm ZestMoney commercial agreement status and target signing date *(Owner: Rajesh Mehta)*
- **[OQ-002]** Define real-time SAP inventory sync interval SLA with RetailCorp IT *(Owner: Rajesh Mehta)*
- **[OQ-003]** Confirm approved cloud provider(s) meeting India data residency requirement *(Owner: Priya Nair)*
- **[OQ-004]** Clarify return eligibility window policy for customer returns *(Owner: Rajesh Mehta)*
- **[OQ-005]** Confirm third-party marketing platform selection for email/SMS campaigns *(Owner: Rajesh Mehta)*
